safest_plan.entry_basis: Zero live 5 Jun: 're-accumulate ~20% lower into a sensible demand area' (20% below the ~8,228 last ≈ 6,580); the chart pins this to the deeper green demand zone ~6,600–6,900 and the 0.786 fib band ~6,500. Note: chart is the Mexican-peso (BMV) listing, so the level is in MXN, not the USD NYSE quote.

targets.basis: Lower edge of the overhead red supply zone on the chart (~9,200) — first target on a successful re-accumulation (estimated; Zero gave no explicit targets)
Top of the range / upper supply line labelled on the chart (9,645; ~9,572 in the second frame) (estimated)

safest_plan.stop_basis: Below the 0.786 fib band (~6,500) and the deeper demand zone (~6,600–6,900); losing ~6,400 breaks the re-accumulation thesis and confirms the downtrend continuing (estimated — Zero gave no explicit stop)

stance_by_source.summary: Clean downtrend with lower highs and lower lows, supply overhead, no break of structure — nothing to trade now; would only add ~20% lower into a demand area to re-accumulate spot/long. Track, don't trade.

conflicts: The chart is the Mexican Bolsa (BMV) listing priced in pesos (~8,228 MXN), not the USD NYSE quote BRK-B normally trades at (~$480) — all levels here are in MXN and must not be read as dollar prices.
The two chart frames label the overhead supply line slightly differently (9,645 vs ~9,572) — same red supply band ~9,200–9,650, treat as a zone not a single price.

The Zero text only said 'a sensible demand area ~20% lower' with no number; the concrete buy zone (~6,600–6,900) and 0.786 fib (~6,500) were added from the chart, which is why the entry is marked as inferred.
plain_english: On the chart Zero used (Berkshire's Mexico listing, in pesos), the stock has been falling in steady steps since early 2025 — each bounce peaks lower than the last — and there's a wall of selling overhead around 9,200–9,650. Zero's own call on 5 June was blunt: nothing to trade here yet, just watch it. His only plan is to buy back in if it drops roughly 20% more into a deeper support area near 6,500–6,900. Do nothing for now: keep this on a watchlist and only consider buying if and when it reaches that lower zone and stops falling.
